summary: High‑quality analog‑like recording captures a solid opening‑night show with standout Althea and guest saxophone flair.
review: The three reviewers praise the tape for its live, analog‑style clarity that makes the listening experience feel like being in the hall. The concert itself is described as a nice opening night of the New Year’s run, highlighted by the presence of Clarence Clemons on sax and an especially effective rendition of “Althea.” Aside from one terse zero‑star comment, sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed, high‑rating reviews (5 and 4 stars) note excellent sound quality and a strong performance; the third review provides no substantive criticism, resulting in an overall rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers comment that the recording conveys the live experience with analog‑like clarity and a realistic soundstage, despite no explicit mention of source (soundboard/audience).

show_quality.setlist_flow: Opening night of the New Year’s run described as “nice sets” with a solid flow; guest sax adds extra color.
